correction_notes: CORRECTED SCORE. Original V18 JSON (score_claude_V18_001) incorrectly listed video_filename as 'V_8.mov' and estimated 145s/451 FLS. ChatGPT native video analysis (37min thinking, 0.64 confidence) placed completion at 162.9s/433.6 FLS. Claude frame review confirms ChatGPT timing is more accurate. V_8.mov is a DIFFERENT video (149s, SURG20 wall-mount) now scored separately as V_8_video.
recording_notes: Phone filming desk-mounted RCA monitor at SURG25 station. Pegboard with instruments visible to left. 'SURG 25 - FLS Circle Cut Training and Testing' instruction sheet pinned above monitor. Pink/red foam block with Penrose drain. Dark (black) suture. 170s total video duration. SV-2 label appears on monitor intermittently.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters field from left. TIMING STARTS ~3s absolute. Needle and suture visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled entry. SURG25 desk RCA station - pegboard, instruction sheet, different setup from SURG20.

frame_analyses.description: Needle being reoriented near drain. Several small regrasp maneuvers.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle loading deliberate rather than fluid - costs ~15s total (ChatGPT frame anchor).

frame_analyses.description: Needle tip engaged at target area, first pass underway through drain tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle acceptable. Tissue handling gentle without visible tearing.

frame_analyses.description: Needle has traversed drain. Suture being drawn through second side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pass appears close to target marks. Placement completing.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being configured with double-wrap appearance before cinching.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot setup. Slowed by regrasping but correct in concept.

frame_analyses.description: First throw seated down onto drain. Approximation maintained.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot lies down reasonably well without obvious loosening.

frame_analyses.description: Alternating loop formed for second throw. Suture wrapping visible above instruments.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternating direction preserved. Setup prolonged - not economical.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being cinched. Both instruments applying tension across knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot control stable. Substantial extra motion before fully seated. Second throw is 47.9s - major time sink.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being formed after additional tail repositioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw segment is slowest part (49.3s). Repeated regrasping before advancement.

frame_analyses.description: Knot stack seated. Tails oriented for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure. No obvious unraveling.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting instrument dividing remaining suture tails inside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting completed intrabody. Quick cut phase at 6.7s - fastest cutting in the series.

knot_assessments.notes: Right-led double throw consistent with surgeon's knot. Moderate confidence (ChatGPT frame-by-frame, confirmed by Claude frame review).
Alternating single throw with left instrument leading. Prolonged but seats without slippage. 47.9s - the primary time bottleneck.
Final single throw alternates back to right. Secure. 49.3s - includes prolonged tail management. Second major time bottleneck.

suture_placement.placement_notes: ChatGPT estimate from native video analysis. Monitor-recorded oblique view limits precision.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Both ChatGPT and Claude frame review confirm no visible gap. Drain closure appears complete. Drain not avulsed.

technique_summary: V18 at 163 seconds is a technically complete but inefficient run. The main performance limiter is throw efficiency: the second throw (47.9s) and third throw (49.3s) consume ~97 seconds combined - 60% of total task time. These are dominated by repeated regrasping and prolonged tail management rather than fundamental technique failure. The bite itself is acceptable, the knot stack appears secure, and cutting is actually the FASTEST in the series at 6.7s. This suggests the trainee has dramatically improved cutting mechanics but regressed on throw efficiency compared to peak performance (V12: 115s). The SURG25 station is a different setup from SURG20 and the original RCA station, so some equipment adaptation time is expected.
improvement_suggestions: Throws 2+3 at 97s combined are the PRIMARY bottleneck - 60% of total time. Cutting is NOT the problem here (6.7s is fastest ever).
During throws, keep one tail under consistent tension and advance each throw directly to the knot - reduce regrasping pauses.
Needle loading at 14.9s is 3x slower than peak (5s) - suggests unfamiliarity with this station's equipment setup.
The 6.7s cutting time proves fast cutting is achievable - the skill exists, it needs to transfer to throw mechanics.
Practice alternating hand leadership more fluidly to reduce the setup time between throws.
CRITICAL DIAGNOSTIC: This run's bottleneck (throws) is completely different from most other runs (cutting). Equipment/setup may be a factor.
strengths: FASTEST CUTTING in the entire series at 6.7s - proves cutting skill has been mastered
No visible gap - complete drain closure
Knot secure with all three throws in correct sequence
Drain not avulsed - safe tissue handling
Task completed successfully despite being at an unfamiliar station
Cross-validated by ChatGPT native video analysis (37min thinking, 0.64 confidence)

key_observations: 163s / 434 FLS - CORRECTED from original 145s/451. ChatGPT cross-validated.
Throws 2+3 at 97s = 60% of total time. Completely different bottleneck from other runs.
Cutting at 6.7s is FASTEST EVER (previous best was ~26s in V14). Massive breakthrough.
Needle loading at 14.9s is 3x slower than 5s peak - station unfamiliarity.
SURG25 is a third distinct station (after RCA lab and SURG20).
18% improvement from baseline (198s -> 163s) on unfamiliar equipment.
The throw inefficiency pattern (regrasping, prolonged setup) matches ChatGPT's detailed frame analysis.
Original V18 JSON was INCORRECT: wrong filename (V_8.mov), wrong time (145s), wrong bottleneck diagnosis (cutting).

correction_log.reason: Original score used wrong video file. ChatGPT native video analysis provided more accurate timing. Claude frame review confirmed ChatGPT assessment.
